name: ds-tooltip
description: Short, dark-surfaced popover with explanatory text, anchored to a trigger element.

slots.name: default
slots.description: Overrides `text` with richer slotted content when present.

constraints.rationale: No primitive references, no raw values.
Tooltip text must clear AA contrast against its surface in both contexts independently.
First registration establishes the API hash; later drift is a rejection.

provenance.author: ai
provenance.justification: Design decision: the requirement calls for a conventionally dark tooltip surface with light text, but no sem-tier token in tokens.json represents an 'inverted' dark surface — every declared surface token (--sem-color-surface-bg) resolves light in both contexts, and no sem color pairs a dark background with light text as a checked ds.contrastPair. Rejected alternative: using --sem-color-text-primary (near-black) as the tooltip's own foreground against --sem-color-surface-bg, which would produce a light tooltip indistinguishable from the page background it floats over -- rejected as visually non-functional for a popover that must read as elevated content. In the absence of a semantic 'inverted surface' token, reached for --prim-color-gray-900 directly as the dark background. This is flagged here deliberately as a likely token-wall violation rather than silently worked around: it names a real gap in tokens.json (no dark/inverted surface token exists yet) that a human should decide how to fill, rather than an AI definition quietly routing around the wall.
